turns.content:
# Handoff — Reopen & Rebuild the Feedback Platform as a First-Class `feedback-api` Crate

This session reviewed the "content-materialization" roadmap and uncovered a domain-boundary defect that supersedes the G-D "feedback ring" work: an entire feedback subsystem (~1,800 lines) lives as unbounded modules inside `rule-api`, there is **no `feedback-api` crate**, and several `[feedback-api]` tickets were closed `done` certifying a domain that has no crate. The G-D ring compiles and its tests pass, but its edges return DTOs instead of persisting to a store, so the feature is architecturally hollow and unusable. The decision this session: **reopen the design and rebuild feedback as a real crate + store + endpoints with one unified schema**, then re-implement the ring as handlers that close onto that store. Start from [./memory-api](./memory-api). Prefer the CLIs at [./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/README.md](./memory-api/tools/cli/ticket-cli/README.md) and [./memory-api/tools/cli/spec-cli/README.md](./memory-api/tools/cli/spec-cli/README.md); the ticket-mcp server was rebuilt+restarted this session and reports cross-store edges as `cross_workspace_edge` warnings correctly.

## Shorthand And Placeholder Legend

- `<mem-ws>` → memory-api ticket/spec store workspace path: `C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api` (owns G-* and `[feedback-api]` tickets).
- `<root-ws>` → root store workspace path: `C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine` (owns the program umbrella `8a90a63c`).
- `EPIC` → content-materialization epic (see ticket legend).
- `G-D` → feedback-ring workstream (see ticket legend).
- `FEEDBACK-CRATE` → the not-yet-existing `memory-api/crates/feedback-api` target crate.

## Ticket Legend (full UUIDs)

- `EPIC` = `3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c` — [content-materialization][epic] Fill specs, Rust/code policy, and the feedback ring on top of session construction (state `new`)
- `G-D` = `5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d` — [content-materialization][feedback-api] G-D: Close the feedback ring (state `done` — MUST reopen)
- `FB-INBOX` = `b1e9e744-aeac-474a-91d9-07e3a362dc76` — [feedback-api] Feedback inbox, metadata indexing, and deep search (state `ready`)
- `FB-INGEST` = `9c95c1e4-3cdb-428e-b9de-800684651226` — [feedback-api] Event ingestion, metadata normalization, and retention policy (state `done` — SUSPECT: no crate exists)
- `FB-CURATION` = `c7542933-3052-45c8-99e6-3e09f40cc9b9` — [feedback-api] Core curation surface — URN usage counting + entity ratings (state `done` — SUSPECT)
- `FB-RULE-RATINGS` = `88800b2e-74f5-4d65-958a-1423d18072e3` — Attach ratings and feedback to rule entries (state `done`)
- `FB-GOVERNANCE` = `4f86d3d2-2b2a-4c9d-9d46-5f2a437f91b7` — [feedback-api] Privileged feedback governance and abuse-boundary enforcement (state `ready`)
- `FB-CLUSTERING` = `b7b84c10-8dc5-4087-87ad-6fe27ebbcd45` — [feedback-api] High-scale search, clustering, and reconciliation workflows (state `ready`)
- `FB-RETENTION` = `c2d6a14a-98b7-4f98-9f62-90a5ccf06d9e` — [feedback-api] Retention, redaction, and privacy incident controls (state `ready`)
- `FB-SLO` = `3a1ec9f8-15ea-43f2-b6d3-89b88cbdcb17` — [feedback-api] Search latency and index growth SLOs (state `ready`)
- `FB-SPEC-ENTITY` = `29bf9628-1dc5-4bb4-ae00-b7410dd52db5` — [spec-api] Add direct feedback on spec entities with integration tests (state `new`)
- `PROGRAM-UMBRELLA` = `8a90a63c-0a07-439f-90e8-9124212b2dc8` — [program][multi-store] Store expansion and operational health program (in `<root-ws>`, state `new`; a G-D depends_on — cross-store edge)

## Long-horizon goal

- `EPIC` `3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c` — materialize real content and close the feedback ring so the system improves itself. The policy tier (G-A/G-B/G-C) is done; the remaining blocker to a legitimate epic close is a **real feedback platform**, not the current `rule-api`-squatting ring.

## Findings

- No `feedback-api` crate exists. Crates under [./memory-api/crates](memory-api/crates) are: audit-api, doc-api, log-api, memory-api, memory-fixtures, memory-matrix, rule-api, session-api, spec-api, test-api, ticket-vscode-core.
- The feedback subsystem is embedded in `rule-api`: [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback.rs](memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback.rs) (679), [feedback_store.rs](memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback_store.rs) (325), [feedback_io.rs](memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback_io.rs) (185), [feedback_tests.rs](memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback_tests.rs) (319), wired via [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/lib.rs](memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/lib.rs).
- `EntityFeedbackStore` in [feedback_store.rs](memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback_store.rs#L3) is salvageable — NDJSON append store with `record_usage`/`record_rating`/`ingest_usage`/`ingest_rating`, `IngestAuthor` classification, `EntityUrn` targeting, `FeedbackRating` (helpful/mixed/not-helpful), `FeedbackNoteKind` (note/suggestion). Extract it, don't rewrite from zero.
- G-D's [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs](memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs) is now de-mocked (real `spec_api::SpecStore`, `test_api::{TestStoreConfig, ValidationOutcome}`, `session_api::{SessionTurn, SessionRole}`, `ticket_api::storage::TicketStore`) and 81 rule-api tests pass — but its edges (`mine_transcript_for_rule_confusion`, `handle_missing_rule_match`, `process_frontend_feedback`) return structs and never write to `EntityFeedbackStore`. Store-less ring.
- No unified feedback schema: `EntityUsageEvent`/`EntityRatingEvent` plus ad-hoc ring DTOs (`MiningFeedbackResult`, `FrontendFeedbackSubmission`) never unify. No `source` discriminant, provenance, status lifecycle, or schema version.
- `FB-INGEST` `9c95c1e4` and `FB-CURATION` `c7542933` are `done` despite the crate they name not existing — closures are suspect and should be audited/reopened.

## Decisions

- Rebuild feedback as `FEEDBACK-CRATE` (`memory-api/crates/feedback-api`) backed by `memory-api` generic entity storage, mirroring `test-api`/`spec-api`/`ticket-api`. Invert coupling: `rule-api` depends on `feedback-api` for rule ratings, not the reverse.
- One versioned `FeedbackEntry` schema: `id`, `schema_version`, `source` (agent | user | frontend | transcript-mined | system), `target: EntityUrn`, `rating`/`note`/`note_kind`, `provenance` (session_id, author, executed_at), `status` (new | triaged | actioned | dismissed). Usage events remain a separate record in the same store.
- Ring edges become services that persist a `FeedbackEntry` (or file a ticket via `ticket-api`) and return the persisted record — no bare DTO returns.
- Ticket-store reads/writes for these ids use `<mem-ws>` (except `PROGRAM-UMBRELLA` `8a90a63c`, which lives in `<root-ws>`).

## Blockers / risks

- **Dirty, uncommitted working tree from prior turns** — the deepest-first commit was never completed. Inside the `memory-api` submodule, `ring.rs`/`Cargo.toml`/`lib.rs` and all `.ticket/tickets/*` edits are uncommitted; `memory-viewers` has an internal commit `d1fd3db` (viewer-api nested submodule removed) whose parent pointer is not yet bumped; root `.spec/specs/71b81a55/body.md` and `.test/default/*` are uncommitted. Do NOT `git add` submodule-internal paths from root — commit inside `memory-api` and `memory-viewers` first, then bump pointers. Keep `Cargo.lock` and `.session/` unstaged.
- G-E `7f7ef435-0504-40cf-8d6c-dd96477a0223` is separately `in-implementation` (real de-submodularization still unfinished: root `.gitmodules` still lists all 4 submodules); not part of this feedback track but shares the dirty tree.

## Board / session note

- Board is empty (no active WIP, no exclusions). Check in on `G-D` before implementing.

## Next actions (execute in order)

1. **Reopen the design.** Revert `G-D` `5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d` off `done` (`update --undo` or `revert --to <rev>`); `EPIC` `3be95a71-f6d7-4c83-b877-1d236751a12c` is already `new`. Create a `[feedback-api][design]` ticket in `<mem-ws>` for the crate boundary + `FeedbackEntry` schema, and audit `FB-INGEST` `9c95c1e4-3cdb-428e-b9de-800684651226` and `FB-CURATION` `c7542933-3052-45c8-99e6-3e09f40cc9b9` (reopen if the `done` state is unsupported by a crate).
2. **Scaffold `FEEDBACK-CRATE`** at `memory-api/crates/feedback-api`; add to the workspace; back it with `memory-api` storage.
3. **Move** `feedback.rs`/`feedback_store.rs`/`feedback_io.rs`/`feedback_tests.rs` out of `rule-api` into `feedback-api`; make `rule-api` depend on `feedback-api` for rule ratings.
4. **Define the versioned `FeedbackEntry` schema** (source discriminant, target URN, rating/note, provenance, status) unifying usage + rating + mined + frontend feedback.
5. **Add transport endpoints** — `feedback-cli` / `feedback-mcp` / `feedback-http` with `ingest`, `inbox`/`query`, `mine`, `summary`, mirroring `test-api`/`ticket-api`.
6. **Re-implement the ring** (from `ring.rs`) as handlers over the store: execution→verified emits a `system` entry + recomputes spec `verified`; transcript mining **persists** `transcript-mined` entries; no-match calls `ticket-api` to file a missing-rule ticket and records a linking entry; frontend/user feedback calls `ingest_rating`.
7. **Author a `feedback-api` spec (contract v2)** — motivation, dependent-expectation, guards (real `test-api` validation specs that write→read the store), positions — governed by a rule via the G-C mechanism. Shrink `G-D` to "wire ring on feedback-api" referencing `FB-INBOX` `b1e9e744` and `FB-INGEST` `9c95c1e4`.
8. **First validation check:** after step 3, run `cargo test -p feedback-api` and confirm `cargo build` of the workspace still resolves with `rule-api` depending on `feedback-api` (watch for dependency cycles: `feedback-api` must not depend back on `rule-api`).

## Completion status

- Action 1 (reopen design): `not-started` — G-D still `done`.
- Action 2 (scaffold crate): `not-started`.
- Action 3 (move modules): `not-started`.
- Action 4 (schema): `not-started`.
- Action 5 (endpoints): `not-started`.
- Action 6 (ring handlers): `not-started` — ring exists in `rule-api` but store-less.
- Action 7 (spec + rule): `not-started`.
- Action 8 (validation): `not-started`.

## Definition of done

- `feedback-api` exists as a first-class crate + filesystem-backed store; `rule-api` depends on it (no reverse coupling, no cycle).
- A single versioned `FeedbackEntry` schema unifies agent/user/frontend/transcript-mined/system feedback with provenance and status.
- Ingest/inbox/mine/summary endpoints exist across CLI/MCP/HTTP.
- The ring edges persist feedback (or file tickets) and are covered by real store-round-trip validation specs; `cargo test -p feedback-api` passes and the workspace builds.
- A `feedback-api` contract-v2 spec is authored and governed by a rule; `G-D` is rescoped to wiring and references existing feedback-api tickets instead of duplicating them; suspect `done` closures (`9c95c1e4`, `c7542933`) are reconciled.
- Working tree committed deepest-first (`memory-viewers` → `memory-api` → root pointer bumps), with `Cargo.lock`/`.session/` left unstaged and no unrelated `.test` evidence touched.

Execute the next actions in order and continue until all are completed or explicitly blocked.

I’ll execute this in the requested order starting with ticket/board/spec hygiene, then scaffold and migrate `feedback-api`, then wire transports and ring persistence, and finally validate builds/tests; first I’m checking board/ticket state in the correct workspace stores so I can safely reopen and check in before edits.I’m running a quick orientation batch now to confirm board/ticket state and current git dirtiness in root plus `memory-api`, then I’ll reopen `G-D` and check in before touching code.

cd /c/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api && cat > /tmp/feedback-contract-v2.md <<'EOF'
<!-- aligned-structure:v2 -->

# Summary

Define a first-class feedback platform contract where `feedback-api` owns persistent, versioned feedback records and `rule-api` consumes it for rule ratings and ring automation.

## Motivation ("why")

The current feedback subsystem was embedded inside `rule-api`, leaving no explicit crate boundary for ingestion/query/governance and enabling done-state tickets to certify a domain that had no owning crate. This contract establishes `feedback-api` as the canonical store and schema owner so dependents can rely on durable feedback behavior.

## Dependent expectation

If this spec is implemented, dependents can rely on a first-class `feedback-api` crate exposing a single versioned `FeedbackEntry` contract with persisted ingestion, inbox/query access, summary aggregation, and ring persistence semantics (`execution->verified`, transcript-mined persistence, missing-rule linkage, frontend/user ingestion).

## Guards

The verification of this specification contract is gated by:
- `val-feedback-api-store-roundtrip` (ensures ingest/write/read round-trips for `FeedbackEntry`, usage, and rating events)
- `val-feedback-api-ring-persistence` (ensures ring handlers persist feedback artifacts instead of returning DTO-only results)
- `val-feedback-api-transport-surface` (ensures CLI, MCP, and HTTP entrypoints expose ingest, inbox/query, mine, and summary paths)

## Positions

- Core schema + store ownership: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs](./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/lib.rs)
- Persistent NDJSON store operations: `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/feedback_store.rs](./memory-api/crates/feedback-api/src/feedback_store.rs)
- Rule API dependency inversion (`rule-api -> feedback-api`): `implemented` at [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback.rs](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/feedback.rs)
- Ring persistence handlers over store: `partial` at [./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs](./memory-api/crates/rule-api/src/ring.rs)
- CLI transport: `implemented` at [./memory-api/tools/cli/feedback-cli/src/main.rs](./memory-api/tools/cli/feedback-cli/src/main.rs)
- MCP transport: `implemented` at [./memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs](./memory-api/tools/mcp/feedback-mcp/src/server.rs)
- HTTP transport: `implemented` at [./memory-api/tools/http/feedback-http/src/lib.rs](./memory-api/tools/http/feedback-http/src/lib.rs)

## Governing-rule requirement

This specification is governed and introduced by:
- [shared/instructions/spec-system/spec-system-guidance/spec-authoring-workflow/structure-the-spec/l52](shared/instructions/spec-system/spec-system-guidance/spec-authoring-workflow/structure-the-spec/l52)

## Traceability

- [5d4078fa G-D wiring](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/ticket.toml)
- [b1e9e744 FB-INBOX](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/b1e9e744-aeac-474a-91d9-07e3a362dc76/ticket.toml)
- [9c95c1e4 FB-INGEST](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/9c95c1e4-3cdb-428e-b9de-800684651226/ticket.toml)
- [c7542933 FB-CURATION](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/c7542933-3052-45c8-99e6-3e09f40cc9b9/ticket.toml)
- [fb6aa078 feedback-api design](C:/Users/linus/git/graph_app/context-engine/memory-api/.ticket/tickets/fb6aa078-1f8b-4a76-99bd-3a26190b1208/ticket.toml)
EOF

turns.content: I found one remaining blocker: the cross-store `G-D -> PROGRAM-UMBRELLA` edge cannot be re-added from `<mem-ws>` (`entity not found`), so I’m documenting that and completing guard traceability by recording the three validation specs referenced in the new feedback contract.

Implemented the handoff plan through crate extraction, ring persistence wiring, transport scaffolding, spec/ticket reconciliation, and validation.

What I changed

1. Reopened design and audited suspect done tickets
- Reopened G-D from done to in-review and checked in on board.
- Reopened both suspect done feedback tickets:
  - [memory-api/.ticket/tickets/9c95c1e4-3cdb-428e-b9de-800684651226/ticket.toml](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/9c95c1e4-3cdb-428e-b9de-800684651226/ticket.toml)
  - [memory-api/.ticket/tickets/c7542933-3052-45c8-99e6-3e09f40cc9b9/ticket.toml](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/c7542933-3052-45c8-99e6-3e09f40cc9b9/ticket.toml)
- Created design ticket and moved it to in-implementation:
  - [memory-api/.ticket/tickets/fb6aa078-1f8b-4a76-99bd-3a26190b1208/ticket.toml](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/fb6aa078-1f8b-4a76-99bd-3a26190b1208/ticket.toml)
- Rescoped G-D description to wiring-only scope:
  - [memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/description.md](memory-api/.ticket/tickets/5d4078fa-d7eb-4f0d-bf84-e21029f5ad5d/description.md)
